raw_consumer_statement:
Subject : Unfair denial of billing dispute regarding defective product purchase ( {$510.00} Best Buy / Citibank ) What happened : On XX/XX/year>, I purchased from Best Buy ( XXXXXXXX CA  ) an electric scooter XXXX XXXX XXXX ( Model XXXX, XXXX XXXX ), which comes with a two-year manufacturers warranty. 

From the very first use, the scooter produced a faint whistling noise that progressively became louder, accompanied by vibration at higher speeds. On XX/XX/year>, I personally visited the same Best Buy store and requested to return or replace the scooter under the manufacturers warranty. The store manager refused, stating that I did not purchase an additional Best Buy Protection Plan which is not legally required for warranty claims under California law. 

Following this incident, I sent an official written complaint by email to the stores management, citing the California Civil Code 1792, 1792.1, and 1793.2, as well as the Song-Beverly Consumer Warranty Act, which obligates retail sellers to accept and facilitate repairs or replacements for defective products covered by manufacturer warranties. Despite that, Best Buy never responded to my email, and the defective product remains unresolved. 

Because the merchant failed to comply, I filed a billing dispute with Best Buy Credit Services ( Citibank N.A. ) for the purchase amount of {$510.00}. However, on XX/XX/year>, the bank sent me a letter stating that the dispute was denied, claiming : The merchants cancellation/return policy was not followed. 
Were unable to determine that a billing error occurred. 
We received a request to withdraw/cancel this dispute. 

These statements are inaccurate. I followed all proper procedures, provided documentation, and never withdrew my dispute. The banks investigation appears incomplete and one-sided, ignoring the fact that the merchant violated state warranty laws and failed to honor the manufacturers warranty.

I am requesting CFPBs assistance in reviewing this case and ensuring a fair resolution under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) and Californias Song-Beverly Consumer Warranty Act.

Company involved : Best Buy Credit Services ( Citibank N.A. ) Merchant : Best Buy ( XXXX, California ) Amount disputed : {$510.00} Date of purchase : XX/XX/year> Date dispute denied : XX/XX/year>XXXX XXXX
